2605.03871.title: EvoLM: Self-Evolving Language Models through Co-Evolved Discriminative Rubrics
2605.03871.paper_summary: Language models encode substantial evaluative knowledge from pretraining, yet current post-training methods rely on external supervision (human annotations, proprietary models, or scalar reward models) to produce reward signals. Each imposes a ceiling. Human judgment cannot supervise capabilities beyond its own, proprietary APIs create dependencies, and verifiable rewards cover only domains with ground-truth answers. Self-improvement from a model's own evaluative capacity is a reward source that scales with the model itself, yet remains largely untapped by current methods. We introduce EVOLM, a post-training method that structures this capacity into explicit discriminative rubrics and uses them as training signal. EVOLM trains two capabilities within a single language model in alternation: (1) a rubric generator producing instance-specific evaluation criteria optimized for discriminative utility, which maximizes a small frozen judge's ability to distinguish preferred from dispreferred responses; and (2) a policy trained using those rubric-conditioned scores as reward. All preference signals are constructed from the policy's own outputs via temporal contrast with earlier checkpoints, requiring no human annotation or external supervision. EVOLM trains a Qwen3-8B model to generate rubrics that outperform GPT-4.1 on RewardBench-2 by 25.7%. The co-trained policy achieves 69.3% average on the OLMo3-Adapt suite, outperforming policies trained with GPT-4.1 prompted rubrics by 3.9% and with the state-of-the-art 8B reward model SkyWork-RM by 16%. Overall, EVOLM demonstrates that structuring a model's evaluative capacity into co-evolving discriminative rubrics enables self-improvement without external supervision.
2605.03871.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型在預訓練中編碼了大量的評估知識，然而當前的後訓練方法依賴於外部監督（人類註釋、專有模型或標量獎勵模型）來產生獎勵信號。每種方法都設置了上限。人類判斷無法監督超出其自身的能力，專有API會產生依賴性，而可驗證的獎勵僅涵蓋具有真實答案的領域。模型自身的評估能力所產生的自我改進是一種隨著模型本身擴展的獎勵來源，但目前的方法仍然在很大程度上未被開發。我們介紹了EVOLM，一種將這種能力結構化為明確的區分標準並用作訓練信號的後訓練方法。EVOLM在單一語言模型中交替訓練兩種能力：（1）生成器產生針對區分效用優化的實例特定評估標準，最大化一個小型凍結評判者區分偏好與不偏好反應的能力；（2）使用這些基於標準的分數作為獎勵進行訓練的策略。所有偏好信號都是通過與早期檢查點的時間對比，從策略自身的輸出構建的，無需人類註釋或外部監督。EVOLM訓練了一個Qwen3-8B模型，生成的標準在RewardBench-2上超越了GPT-4.1達25.7%。共同訓練的策略在OLMo3-Adapt套件上達到69.3%的平均值，超越了使用GPT-4.1提示的標準訓練的策略3.9%以及使用最先進的8B獎勵模型SkyWork-RM的策略16%。總體而言，EVOLM展示了將模型的評估能力結構化為共同演化的區分標準，使得在無需外部監督的情況下實現自我改進。
2605.03871.author: Shuyue Stella Li et.al.
2605.03871.authors: Shuyue Stella Li, Rui Xin, Teng Xiao, Yike Wang, Rulin Shao, Zoey Hao, Melanie Sclar, Sewoong Oh, Faeze Brahman, Pang Wei Koh, Yulia Tsvetkov
